data.title: Luca Mining COO Ramon Mendoza sheds interim tag
data.description:
Luca Mining (OTCQX:LUCMF) announces that Ramon Mendoza, currently Luca's interim COO, will assume the position of permanent COO effective as of June 01, 2025. He will also continue to be the company's Chief Technical Officer.

Mendoza has over 35 years of experience in the mining industry, focusing on mine development and process optimization. He successfully improved gold and metal production at Luca's Campo Morado and Tahuehueto mines in Mexico.

The company also announces that David Rhodes will be stepping down as Chairman effective as of June 10, 2025, however he will remain a valued member of Luca's board.

Peter Damouni will step into the role of Chairman of the Board. He has served as an executive and director of several public companies listed on the TSX, TSXV, and LSE.

data.title: Sachem Capital announces private placement of $100M senior secured notes
data.description:
Real estate lender Sachem Capital (NYSE:SACH) announced on Thursday a private placement of $100M five-year senior secured notes due June 11, 2030, to various institutional investors.

The company said an initial draw of $50M was made at closing, and the remaining $50M must be drawn by May 15, 2026.

The notes carry an interest at a fixed rate of 9.875% per annum, payable quarterly, and a commitment fee of 1.0% on the undrawn portion.

The payment due on the notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed by the company.

The proceeds are intended to be used for a combination of purposes, including the repayment of existing facility balances, the origination of new investments, and to redeem the 7.75% unsecured notes maturing in September.

data.title: ADC Therapeutics announces $100M private financing
data.description:
• ADC Therapeutics (NYSE:ADCT) has entered into securities purchase agreements for the sale of its equity securities to certain institutional investors in a $100M private investment in public equity ("PIPE”) financing.
  • The company is selling 13M common shares at $3.53 a share and pre-funded warrants to purchase 15.7M common shares at $3.43 per pre-funded warrant.
  • Gross proceeds from the financing are anticipated to be ~$100M, extending the company’s expected cash runway into 2028.
  • ADC Therapeutics plans to use the net proceeds to fund multiple catalysts primarily in support of ZYNLONTA clinical development as well as ZYNLONTA commercialization activities, working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The PIPE is expected to close on June 16, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: America's Car-Mart GAAP EPS of $1.26 beats by $0.40, revenue of $370.2M beats by $26.7M
data.description:
• America's Car-Mart press release (NASDAQ:CRMT): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.26 beats by $0.40.
  • Revenue of $370.2M (+1.5% Y/Y) beats by $26.7M.
  • Sales volumes increased 2.6% to 15,649 units
  • Allowance for credit loss improved to 23.25%, compared to 24.31% at January 31, 2025, and 25.32% at April 30, 2024
  • Net charge-offs as a % of average finance receivables improved to 6.9% vs. 7.3%

data.title: Genius Sports attracts a new bull rating from Texas Capital
data.description:
Texas Capital called Genius Sports Limited (NYSE:GENI) one of the most dynamic companies in the gaming sector as it started off coverage with a Buy rating. 

Analyst David Bain wrote that Genius Sports Limited (NYSE:GENI) is a top pure play, real-money online stock pick based on its mix of real-time sports data collection, betting technology, media distribution, focused stadium, and strong broadcasting technology position.

"We believe GENI has created moated sports technology with profitable tentacles positioned throughout several growing Total Addressable Markets totaling hundreds of billions of dollars," advised Bain. The firm thinks Genius Sports (GENI) could generate revenue growth of at least ~20% for the next several years, and see margins should expand each year. "Further, we believe GENI’s valuation will bifurcate from higher valued gaming equities to established SaaS data providers with significant barriers to entry and large TAMs," he highlighted.

Crunching the numbers on GENI, Bain said that expanding flow-through from scale, additional price increases, and a new high-margin media offering should give investors confidence that EBITDA consensus expectations are conservative.

Texas Capital assigned a price target to Genius Sports (GENI) of $14, which represents more than 40% upside from Wednesday's closing price.

The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on GENI is also flashing Buy.

data.title: European indexes drop on renewed trade tensions, yields sink
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET. 

London (UKX) -0.15% to 8,853, UK's economy contracts as UK's trade deficit widens in April. Industrial production in the UK declined by 0.6% M/M in April. The British pound hovered around $1.35, near its highest level in three years, supported by broad-based dollar weakness.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.99% to 23,710.

France (CAC:IND) -0.70% to 7,722.

Finland's current account deficit narrowed sharply to €222M in April.

The annual inflation rate in the Netherlands stood at 3.3% in May.

The annual inflation rate in Portugal was confirmed at a three-month high of 2.3% in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) dropped 0.80% to 547.2, and almost all sectors in the red led by travel. Investor sentiment gripped by trade uncertainties as President Donald announced plans to send letters to key trading partners within the next one to two weeks. 

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the charts today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down less than 4 basis points to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 7 basis points to 2.47%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 6 basis points to 4.49%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Marqeta to power Klarna Card in the U.S.
data.description:
Card issuing platform Marqeta (NASDAQ:MQ) said on Thursday that it is working with the global digital bank Klarna (KLAR) to power the Klarna Card in the U.S.

Earlier this month, Klarna announced a pilot launch of its new card, which combines debit and pay-over-time features, in collaboration with Visa (V) (VISA:CA).

A broader rollout of the product in the U.S. and Europe is expected later this year.

Marqeta has been powering Klarna's virtual cards in the U.S. since 2018.

data.title: The Baldwin Insurance subsidiary to buy Hippo’s homebuilder distribution network.
data.description:
• The Baldwin Group (NASDAQ:BWIN) subsidiary Westwood Insurance Agency to acquire Hippo’s (NYSE:HIPO) homebuilder distribution network.
  • This acquisition will add eight new homebuilders to Westwood’s client roster, expanding its coverage to 20 of the top 25 homebuilders in the United States, representing over 35% of total new single-family homes built annually
  • Hippo’s homebuilder distribution network generated revenues of approximately $29.2 million in the most recent trailing 12-month period and is expected to deliver approximately $7 million of adjusted EBITDA over the 12 months.

data.title: Cenovus Energy restores full production at Christina Lake oil sands
data.description:
Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) said Thursday it has restarted production at its Christina Lake oil sands facility in Alberta, following a temporary shutdown due to wildfire activity in the area.

Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) said it restarted Christina Lake operations on June 3 and production had ramped up over the course of the week.

The company also said there had been no damages to its infrastructure and it continues to monitor the overall wildfire situation in Alberta.

MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) said Wednesday that it was ramping up to full operations at its own Christina Lake site following wildfire activity.

data.title: BlackRock sets 2030 aspirations in crypto, private markets, others
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), the largest asset manager in the world, is seeking to become the largest crypto asset manager globally as well as boost its private markets business, the company said Thursday in its Investor Day slides.

The company targets at least $50B crypto assets under management in the next 15 years. It seeks to leverage its experience in the U.S. to expand digital assets exchange-traded products into Europe and Canada.

Private markets form an important part of its overall growth strategy. In the private markets-to-insurance channel, BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) seeks to become the largest third-party manager in balance sheet insurance assets with $700B in assets under management in 2030.

It targets $400B of cumulative fundraising in private markets by 2030.

Its expansion in private markets, digital assets, and active ETFs are intended to drive positive leverage to BlackRock's (BLK) fee rate, said Martin Small, chief financial officer and global head of corporate strategy.

For the company overall, BlackRock (BLK) is targeting doubling its market capitalization to $280B from ~$140B in 2024. It's aiming for a compounded annual growth rate for revenue of 10%, bringing revenue to $35B in 2030 from $20B in 2024, the company said.

It set its adjusted operating income target for $15B vs. $8B achieved in 2024.

BlackRock (BLK) stock slipped 0.6% in Thursday premarket trading.
